revision: iter-2 — BA-QA 7 findings + codex F1-F12 incorporated
ac_uid_algorithm: sha256(type + given + when + then + json.dumps(check, sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=False))[:16]

items.given: All 13 tests/generated/20260520-221452/test_AC_*.py files exist (live Glob count must equal 13)
items.when: Each file is read AND pytest tests/generated/20260520-221452/ -q is run
items.then: Zero files contain bare 'pass' as sole body; zero contain 'TEST_INCOMPLETE:' sentinel; zero contain vacuous assertions; zero use pytest.skip or @pytest.mark.skip; each test body references the target file/path/symbol named in its docstring GIVEN clause; each test body contains at least one assertion exercising the THEN clause specific predicate

items.when: The file is grepped for the heading anchor '^#### Active CJK Detection Algorithm$' AND the body following the heading (until next #### or ###) is extracted
items.then: Heading exists exactly once; heading appears AFTER current line 318 Unconditional-scan paragraph and BEFORE current line 320 Exemption paragraph; body contains numbered MUST-list (>=4 steps) specifying scan-before-exemption ordering; body contains verbatim positive invariant phrase 'MUST NOT skip CJK scanning for any in-scope file'

check.body_extraction: lines from heading anchor through next ^####|^### heading
check.body_must_contain_phrase: MUST NOT skip CJK scanning for any in-scope file
check.literal_skip_detection_ban_RETRACTED: iteration-1 banned 'skip detection' literally; iter-2 retracts because the positive invariant contains 'MUST NOT skip CJK scanning' which is semantically equivalent — codex F2

items.given: agents/ba.md is read AND docs/dev/context-20260524-122910.json is parsed
items.when: Quality Standards checklist + JSON-context schema are inspected AND jq queries run against context JSON
items.then: agents/ba.md Quality Standards contains the exact checklist line; agents/ba.md JSON-context schema documents test_writer_expected as required boolean with formula; context.json top-level test_writer_expected is type=boolean AND value equals the formula evaluated on this context's complexity_tier and risk_level

check.checklist_exact_match: - [ ] test_writer_expected boolean emitted in context.json: (complexity_tier in {STANDARD, COMPLEX}) OR (risk_level == "high")

items.given: scripts/blast-radius-tool.py is read AND two deterministic fixtures are executed
items.when: Fixture A: temp git repo with one tracked-modified file + one untracked-non-ignored file; invoke `blast-radius-tool.py --git-diff --base HEAD --output /tmp/br-fixture-a.json --task-id fixture-a` from that repo. Fixture B: temp tree where one source file is textually referenced from >50 sibling files; invoke `blast-radius-tool.py --files <fanout-source> --output /tmp/br-fixture-b.json --task-id fixture-b`
items.then: Fixture A analyzed_files contains both tracked-modified AND untracked-non-ignored, each exactly once; source git_diff_files body contains 'ls-files' + '--others' + '--exclude-standard'; source defines module-level MAX_EDGES_PER_SOURCE_FILE = 50; Fixture B edges where from == fanout-source count is <= 50 AND omitted_edges_count > 0 AND omitted_edges_count == actual_input_reference_count - 50; Fixture A output top-level includes omitted_edges_count as integer; dedup is by (from, to, edge_type) 3-tuple

fixture_a.name: untracked_file_detection
fixture_a.setup: mkdir tempdir && cd tempdir && git init -q && echo 'a' > tracked.txt && git add tracked.txt && git commit -q -m init && echo 'b' >> tracked.txt && echo 'c' > untracked.txt

fixture_b.name: high_fanout_cap_enforcement
fixture_b.setup: mkdir tempfanout && cd tempfanout && echo 'def util(): pass' > util.py && for i in $(seq 1 60); do echo 'from util import util' > caller_$i.py; done
assertions: omitted_edges_count + count(edges where from == 'util.py' AND edge_type matches reference type) == 60_or_higher_total_input_refs

check.dedup_check: for every output JSON, len(edges) == len(set([(e['from'], e['to'], e['edge_type']) for e in edges])) — distinct edge_types for the same (from,to) pair are preserved
check.size_threshold_RETRACTED: iteration-1 required <500KB; iter-2 retracts because non-deterministic. Replaced with deterministic structural bounds above — codex F6/F7/F8

items.when: Heading, body, and pytest invocation gate are inspected
items.then: Exact-match grep finds new heading '#### Phase 5: Manifest Verification (when complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high)' AND old heading '(when test-writer ran upstream)' is absent; Phase 5 body explicitly states phase runs when test_writer_expected == true, computed as (complexity_tier in {STANDARD, COMPLEX}) OR (risk_level == high), even when per-task manifest missing; pytest gate at line 1036 widened from 'When complexity_tier >= STANDARD' to include 'OR risk_level == high'; item 5 at line 1039 preserved; NO marker token tested_trigger_asymmetry_eliminated required

check.new_heading_exact_match_grep: ^#### Phase 5: Manifest Verification \(when complexity_tier >= STANDARD OR risk_level == high\)$
check.marker_token_check_RETRACTED: iteration-1 required 'tested_trigger_asymmetry_eliminated: true' fenced code block; iter-2 retracts as ceremonial proof per codex F4

items.given: scripts/canary-verify.sh verify_write_guard and verify_git_privilege_guard functions are read; canary-verify.sh is executed in a sandboxed environment
items.when: The negative-payload test logic is inspected AND the canary is run
items.then: verify_write_guard contains deny-payload test (Write payload targeting EXISTING file via mktemp; guard blocks overwriting existing files per hooks/pretool-write-guard.sh:120-128) and asserts rc==2; verify_git_privilege_guard contains deny-payload (destructive git command e.g. 'git push --force') and asserts rc==2; both functions emit_failure when deny-payload NOT blocked

check.runtime_check: canary-verify.sh exit code is 0 when all hooks correctly enforce denies

items.given: hooks/doc_sync/regen_readme.py and hooks/doc_sync/tree.py are read AND a fixture tree containing cp-state-foo.json + spec-20260524-test.json is provided
items.when: The skip logic is inspected AND doc_sync is run against the fixture
items.then: Both files contain executable suppression logic (not comments) for BOTH cp-state- prefix AND spec-2026 prefix patterns; generated README and INDEX tree output exclude any file matching either pattern; suppression uses startswith or equivalent prefix-aware filtering (not exact-name set membership)

check.fixture_check: fixture files matching cp-state-* and spec-2026* are absent from generated tree/README output

items.then: jq index for '*/.claude/dev-registry/*/blast-radius-map.json' returns non-null integer; jq index for '*/docs/dev/acceptance-criteria-' returns non-null integer (preservation check); policy_version equals 5 (live baseline 4 + 1); _note field is extended with paragraph mentioning policy_version 5 and BA blast-radius prefix

check.substring_check_RETRACTED: iteration-1 used must_include_substring 'blast-radius-map' which is too loose; iter-2 uses exact-string jq index per codex F10

items.when: All echo and printf calls are inspected via a two-stage check
items.then: Stage 1 collects all lines matching '^[[:space:]]*(echo|printf)'; stage 2 filters to those NOT containing '>&2' or '1>&2'; stage-2 filtered list MUST be empty; trailing comments after redirect allowed; simple regex pattern ending in [^>]$ is FORBIDDEN as the gate because false-positives on lines ending in '2'

affected_files.line_range: 288-306 (Location-complaint tokenization block to replace) + 308-314 (Contract D block to preserve)

items.given: commands/dev.md is read AND the scoped region between heading anchors is extracted via sed
items.when: Scoped region is inspected (sed -n '/^\*\*Location-complaint tokenization procedure\*\* (shared with Contract D):$/,/^-->$/p' commands/dev.md) AND Contract D block at lines 308-314 is read for preservation verification
items.then: Within scoped region: tokens Jaccard, jaccard, set-intersection wording (tokens of, F ∩ C, overlap =), domain-token example table are ABSENT; replacement instructs orchestrator to use semantic LLM / natural-language judgment AND explicitly references ALL THREE of complaint_location_keywords, affected_files, located_source; Contract D paragraph at lines 308-314 (Contract D novelty extension — two dimensions through different-words-same-wrong-file loophole) is PRESERVED VERBATIM byte-for-byte; verification grep for forbidden tokens uses sed-extracted scoped region only

check.scope_extraction_command: sed -n '/^\*\*Location-complaint tokenization procedure\*\* (shared with Contract D):$/,/^-->$/p' commands/dev.md

contract_d_preservation.extraction_command: sed -n '/^\*\*Contract D novelty extension — two dimensions\*\*:$/,/different-words-same-wrong-file" loophole\./p' commands/dev.md
contract_d_preservation.scope_bounded: verbatim preservation is bounded to lines 308-314 (Contract D paragraph), NOT 'onward indefinitely' per codex F5

check.line_range_RETRACTED: iteration-1 used brittle line range 284-303 which was wrong (actual block is 288-306); iter-2 uses heading-anchor sed extraction per BAQA-03
